detail.reasoning: Good role adherence with proper feature assessment structure. Actionable recommendations with specific next steps and labels. Quality suffers from some hedging and verbose explanations. Inappropriately asked too many questions and deferred to research when expert analysis was expected - should have provided defensible assessment of implementation complexity and trade-offs based on standard dark mode patterns.

Strong role adherence with proper assessment structure, clear recommendation, and business outcome focus. Actionable next steps with specific assignees and research priorities. Good quality with structured format and minimal hedging. Appropriately matched complexity with expert analysis, identified key unknowns, and avoided premature implementation recommendations while providing defensible assessment.

Agent failed to perform the core role of assessing the feature request. Instead of conducting expert analysis on AI-powered code review comments (flagging low signal, questioning problem definition, comparing to existing tools like CodeRabbit/Copilot, addressing build vs integrate trade-offs), it asked for information that should be available or made reasonable assumptions. For 'complicated' complexity, the agent should have produced analysis while noting assumptions, not deflected with extensive information gathering.

Agent asks for extensive details when enough info was provided for expert analysis. For issue-feature-review role, should assess severity/priority based on available info (app crashes, >100MB files, 2 reports). While preliminary assessment is decent, the heavy focus on requesting more information rather than providing expert analysis with flagged assumptions doesn't match the complexity level or role expectations.
